title: hymenophyllum batuense
intro: Hymenophyllum batuense is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the basket ferns due to their unique basket-like fronds. The plant is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, where it grows in tropical forests. Hymenophyllum batuense has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Hymenophyllum batuense include the potential treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory infections, and it can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions or decoctions.
